paper.title_en: De Sitter-Core Regular Black Holes and the Boundedness of Hawking Temperature
paper.abstract_en: <jats:p>We study the Hawking temperature of static, asymptotically flat, spherically symmetric regular black holes under assumptions that isolate the geometric effect of a finite-curvature core. Starting from the metric \[ \dd s^2=-f(r)\,\dd t^2+\frac{\dd r^2}{f(r)}+r^2\dd\Omega_2^2, \qquad f(r)=1-\frac{2m(r)}{r}, \] we derive the surface gravity and Hawking temperature directly from the near-horizon geometry. We then show, first, that a smooth de Sitter-type core already removes the Schwarzschild route to arbitrarily large temperature by excluding any sequence of outer horizons with \(r_h\to 0\). Second, under a stronger but transparent hypothesis, namely that the effective energy density stays bounded above by its central de Sitter value, equivalently \(0\le \rho(r)\le 3/(8\pi \ell^2)\), we obtain the explicit ceiling \[ T_H\le \frac{1}{4\pi \ell}. \] Here \(\ell\) is the core curvature radius defined by \(f(r)=1-r^2/\ell^2+O(r^4)\) near the center. The derivation shows exactly where the assumptions enter, and it makes clear that the boundedness result belongs to a broad but still controlled class of regular black holes. The extremal condition emerges as \(1-2m&apos;(r_h)=0\), and in one-parameter families with an extremal endpoint and a large-mass Schwarzschild regime, continuity forces the existence of at least one maximum-temperature point. A short check with the Hayward geometry confirms the general logic and yields \(T_{\max}=1/(18\pi \ell)\), safely below the universal ceiling.</jats:p>
